lessons.title: Timeout Waiting for Selector — Element Not Yet Appeared or Covered
lessons.context: Modern web apps load data asynchronously. Elements appear after API responses complete or loading spinners disappear. Tests that interact with elements before they're ready fail with timeout errors. Always wait for a specific condition, never add a fixed sleep.

problem.error: Timeout waiting for selector / locator timeout
problem.cause: Element not yet appeared (async load) or covered by a loading overlay

antipattern.description: Using fixed sleep to wait for elements
antipattern.why_bad: Fixed sleep is either too short (flaky) or too long (slow) — doesn't adapt to actual load time

solution.description: Wait for a specific condition: loading spinner hidden, API response received, or element visible.
code: // GOOD — wait for loading to disappear

lessons.ai_instruction: Never use waitForTimeout() in generated tests. Always wait for a specific condition: element state change, loading indicator hidden, or API response received.

lessons.title: Element Covered by Loading Overlay — Wait for Spinner to Disappear
lessons.context: Loading spinners sit on top of page content. Even when the target element exists in the DOM, it cannot be interacted with because the overlay intercepts pointer events. Playwright throws 'element is covered by another element' in this case.

problem.error: Element covered by loading overlay / intercepts pointer events
problem.cause: Loading spinner or modal overlay intercepts clicks on the target element

antipattern.description: Clicking element without waiting for overlay to disappear
antipattern.why_bad: The overlay intercepts the click — the action never reaches the target element
code: // BAD — overlay may still be visible

solution.description: Always wait for the loading overlay to disappear before interacting with elements beneath it.
code: // GOOD — wait for spinner to disappear first

lessons.ai_instruction: Before clicking any element that appears after a loading state, always add waitForSelector('.loading-spinner', { state: 'hidden' }) first.

lessons.title: Data Not Loaded — Wait for API Response Before Asserting
lessons.context: Tables and dashboards populate data from API calls made after page load. Asserting on data immediately after navigation often catches the empty/loading state before the API response arrives.

problem.error: Empty table / no data / assertion fails on loading state
problem.cause: API has not responded yet — assertion runs on the empty/loading state

antipattern.description: Asserting on data immediately after navigation without waiting for API
antipattern.why_bad: The API call is async — data may not be present yet when the assertion runs

solution.description: Wait for the specific API response that populates the data before asserting.
code: // GOOD — wait for API response first

lessons.ai_instruction: After navigating to a page that loads data from an API, always add waitForResponse() for the relevant endpoint before asserting on the data.

lessons.title: Navigation Timeout — Page Loads Slowly or Wrong URL
lessons.context: page.goto() has a default timeout that may be too short for slow pages. The default 'load' event also fires before async resources finish loading.

problem.cause: Page loads slowly, has many resources, or the URL is incorrect

antipattern.description: Using default goto() without waitUntil for slow pages
antipattern.why_bad: Default 'load' event fires before all async resources finish — page may still be loading
code: // BAD — may timeout on slow pages

solution.description: Use waitUntil: 'networkidle' for pages with many async resources.
code: // GOOD — wait for network to settle
// GOOD — increase timeout for known slow page

lessons.ai_instruction: For pages that load many async resources, use goto() with waitUntil: 'networkidle'. For known slow pages, also increase the timeout and add a comment explaining why.
